title: Acute Chest Pain - Anteroseptal STEMI
chief_complaint: Severe central chest pain radiating to left arm for 2 hours, associated with shortness of breath and sweating

physical_exam.chest: Clear breath sounds bilaterally, no wheezes or crackles
physical_exam.heart: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs, no rubs
physical_exam.extremities: Warm, no edema, pulses equal bilaterally

hidden_data.cbc: Hemoglobin: 13.5 g/dL, WBC: 8.2 x10⁹/L, Platelets: 250 x10⁹/L
hidden_data.ecg: Sinus tachycardia at 110 bpm. ST-segment elevation of 3mm in leads V1-V4. Reciprocal ST depression in leads II, III, aVF.
hidden_data.cxr: Mild cardiomegaly. No pulmonary edema. No pleural effusion.

key_learning_points: ECG must be obtained within 10 minutes of presentation for any patient with chest pain
ST elevation in V1-V4 indicates anteroseptal wall involvement, typically LAD artery occlusion
Door-to-balloon time goal is less than 90 minutes
Always check right-sided leads (V4R) and posterior leads (V7-V9) if inferior STEMI suspected

patient_responses.pain_onset: It started suddenly about 2 hours ago. I was just sitting watching TV.
patient_responses.pain_character: It's like a heavy weight on my chest, crushing me. It goes to my left shoulder and arm.
patient_responses.pain_severity: On a scale of 1 to 10, it's a 9. It's the worst pain I've ever felt.
patient_responses.associated_symptoms: I feel like I can't catch my breath, and I've been sweating like crazy.
patient_responses.smoking: Yes, I smoke. About a pack a day for the last 30 years.
patient_responses.past_history: I have high blood pressure, but I don't always take my medication. I also have sugar diabetes.
patient_responses.medications: The doctor gave me something for blood pressure and sugar, but I don't remember the names.
patient_responses.family_history: My father died of a heart attack when he was 55. My brother also has heart problems.
patient_responses.allergies: No, I'm not allergic to any medicines as far as I know.
patient_responses.last_meal: I had lunch about 3 hours ago. Just a sandwich.

hints.initial_approach: Start with ABC assessment and obtain vital signs immediately.
hints.ecg_hint: This is the most important initial test. Look at the anterior leads carefully.
hints.troponin_hint: Remember that troponin may be normal in the first 2-4 hours. This patient is at 2 hours.
hints.management_hint: Think about MONA protocol: Morphine, Oxygen, Nitroglycerin, Aspirin. But also prepare for reperfusion.
